Wednesday Racing: Omolong dazzles on race day debut at Warwick Farm

The countdown to the 2026 New Zealand Bloodstock National Weanling Sale is underway, with a compact yet quality catalogue of 114 youngsters (after withdrawals) selling this Thursday 25 June at Karaka.

Two foals from the first crop of Cambridge Stud's dual Group 1-winning shuttler Chaldean will be offered at Thursday's New Zealand Bloodstock National Weanling Sale, and their reception has the Cambridge team enthused for what the future holds for the stallion.

A far-reaching plan released to arrest New Zealand racing's decline was released on Wednesday, and it comes from seven of its most powerful breeders, investors and operators. From a pooled property vehicle to tax reform and a single governing body, the report asks whether those who own racing's assets are ready to put them to work for its future.
Group 2-winning Castelvecchio mare Double Market tops the Inglis Digital June Late Sale for $200,000, where three mares bound for commercial dates in the spring realised six figure price tags.

Milan Park Stud's Tony Rider has announced he has purchased The Oaks Stud and will take over the property at the end of July 2027. He intends to honour Dick Karreman's vision of the farm as a leading Australasian commercial enterprise, while continuing his personal investment into enhancing the nation's breeding population.

When Cape Falls Thoroughbreds' Brett Fogarty purchased multiple stakes winner Paris Petard in 2011, he was hoping to build a dynasty. That plan is gaining serious momentum in 2026 as the mare's granddaughter Hot And High clinches a second stakes victory.

Queensland-based jockey Kyle Wilson-Taylor is set to relocate to New Zealand, targeting spring and summer opportunities. With prior success across the Tasman and established connections, the 26-year-old sees the move as a natural and well-timed progression.

The momentum is building for Yulong stallion Pierata after his son, Midnight Dynamite, nabbed the Listed Civic Stakes at Randwick on Saturday to become the sire’s third stakes winner. As his first Victorian-born youngsters prepare to hit the track, TTR chatted with Harry King about the stallion's surging statistics.
